title: Searchable picker shape contract + project-scoped sources schema
description: Add the additive `searchable-categorized` picker shape and `SearchableSourceCategory` to the shared integration types, widen the roubo.yaml `SourceEntry` object form to carry `project`, `boardMode`, and `mineScope`, add the root `excludedStatusCategories` field (default `["Done"]`), and seed it in the Jira plugin manifest. The JSON schema (`schema/roubo-config.schema.json`) and manifest schema are updated in lockstep. This is the contract foundation every later slice builds on.
acceptance_criteria: shared/integration-types.ts exports the searchable-categorized shape literal and SearchableSourceCategory without altering multi-list or categorized-multi-list
shared/config-schema.ts SourceEntry object form accepts project, boardMode, and mineScope; root accepts excludedStatusCategories
schema/roubo-config.schema.json and shared/plugin-manifest-schema.ts are updated to match in the same change
github-com and ghe plugins continue to validate and render unchanged

title: getSourceOptions RPC + source-options route + typed client hook
description: Implement the `getSourceOptions(category, scope, search, cursor)` plugin RPC as a generalization of the existing getFacetOptions async path: scoped, text-matched, cursor-paginated project/board/filter/epic search against the Jira REST and Agile APIs. Add the host route copied from the facet-options handler and the typed client + infinite-query hook. Removes the instance-wide loaders and the 50-epic cap.
acceptance_criteria: getSourceOptions returns a scoped, cursor-paginated page for project, board, filter, and epic categories
Epic and filter discovery use search endpoints with no fixed item cap and no instance-wide enumeration
A new GET .../integration/source-options route proxies to the plugin and a typed client hook consumes it
Every selectable item is reachable by paging the cursor; no page is dropped or duplicated

title: Async type-ahead source picker with project-first cascade, rich labels, and modal-clip fix
description: Build the debounced (250ms) async-search client control and the searchable source picker arm. Gate board/filter/epic pickers behind project scope (project-first cascade). Render full, untruncated labels with project key + item id. Fix the configure-modal dropdown clipping with a body-level portal popover. Meet WCAG 2.1 AA keyboard navigation.
acceptance_criteria: Board/filter/epic pickers are disabled until at least one Jira project is in scope
The picker debounces input ~250ms and renders results from getSourceOptions with Load more paging
Each result shows the full name plus a monospace project-key + item-id secondary line, never truncated to ambiguity
The dropdown is never clipped by the configure modal, regardless of result-list length
The picker is fully keyboard-navigable with visible focus and screen-reader labels (WCAG 2.1 AA)

title: JQL builder: project/board/mine source kinds, union, and active-sprint resolution
description: Extend the JQL builder to support the new source kinds (project, board, mine) alongside filter and epic, building a union (OR) across per-source clauses. Resolve a board source to its active sprint by default with a widen-to-whole-board option. Implement the assigned-to-me preset in both in-project and anywhere modes via getCurrentUser. Preserve the per-source incremental updated>= watermark across the new kinds.
acceptance_criteria: toSourceClauses handles project, board, and mine in addition to filter and epic
A board source resolves to its active sprint by default and can widen to the whole board
Assigned-to-me supports in-scoped-projects and anywhere modes
The cut list is the de-duplicated union of all configured sources
The per-source updated>= watermark is preserved and advances only at pagination exhaustion

title: Server-side status-category exclusion in JQL + injection hardening
description: Move status exclusion off the client and into the JQL query as the single source of truth, defaulting to excluding Jira's Done status category and honoring a user-editable category set through the three-layer merge. Remove client-side applyFilters exclusion. Harden all user-supplied search terms against JQL injection and ensure no JQL, PAT, search term, or issue content is logged.
acceptance_criteria: Excluded statuses are applied in the JQL (statusCategory not in ...) so closed/done issues never reach a result page
The default excluded set is the Done category and is user-editable; client-side exclusion is removed as source of truth
If statusCategory is unsupported on the instance, the builder falls back to enumerating status names
User search terms are escaped/parameterized; a crafted term cannot inject a clause
Logs, metrics, and traces never carry PAT, JQL, search terms, or issue contents

title: Source-kind translation extension + old-shape clean-break detection
description: Add project/board/mine to the host CATEGORY_TO_KIND translation map (retiring the old boards kind) so configured sources flow to the plugin correctly. Detect legacy boards/epics/filters-shaped configs and prompt the user to re-pick rather than silently honoring them (clean break, no migration). Manifest-driven mapping is deferred to issue #349.
acceptance_criteria: Configured project, board, and mine sources translate to the correct plugin source kind
A legacy old-shape source config is not silently honored; the user is prompted to re-pick in the new picker
No automatic migration of old-shape configs is performed

title: Automate picker end-to-end flows
description: Automate the picker-area end-to-end journeys defined in .specifications/jira-sources-scale/test-cases.json: TC-019 (pick project first), TC-020 (add scrum board active sprint), TC-021 (add saved filter), TC-023 (combine multiple sources), TC-026 (assigned-to-me), TC-027 (epic scope), TC-029 (disambiguate similar names). The automation mirrors each test's steps and runs in CI.
acceptance_criteria: Automated test for TC-019 (project-first scoping) exists, mirrors the steps, and passes in CI.
Automated test for TC-020 (scrum board active sprint) exists, mirrors the steps, and passes in CI.
Automated test for TC-021 (saved filter source) exists, mirrors the steps, and passes in CI.
Automated test for TC-023 (combine multiple sources) exists, mirrors the steps, and passes in CI.
Automated test for TC-026 (assigned-to-me) exists, mirrors the steps, and passes in CI.
Automated test for TC-027 (epic scope) exists, mirrors the steps, and passes in CI.
Automated test for TC-029 (disambiguate similar names) exists, mirrors the steps, and passes in CI.
All listed e2e_flow tests for this area share the automation harness and run as a single CI suite.

title: Automate source-search end-to-end flows

acceptance_criteria: Automated test for TC-022 (search instead of scroll) exists, mirrors the steps, and passes in CI.
All listed e2e_flow tests for this area share the automation harness and run as a single CI suite.

title: Automate cut-list end-to-end flows
description: Automate the cut-list-area end-to-end journeys defined in .specifications/jira-sources-scale/test-cases.json: TC-024 (closed issues never appear) and TC-025 (edit which status categories are excluded). The automation mirrors each test's steps and runs in CI.
acceptance_criteria: Automated test for TC-024 (closed issues kept out) exists, mirrors the steps, and passes in CI.
Automated test for TC-025 (edit excluded status categories) exists, mirrors the steps, and passes in CI.
All listed e2e_flow tests for this area share the automation harness and run as a single CI suite.

title: Automate config end-to-end flows
description: Automate the config-area end-to-end journey defined in .specifications/jira-sources-scale/test-cases.json: TC-028 (a team default is overridden by a personal source set). The automation mirrors the test's steps and runs in CI.
acceptance_criteria: Automated test for TC-028 (team default overridden by personal selection) exists, mirrors the steps, and passes in CI.
All listed e2e_flow tests for this area share the automation harness and run as a single CI suite.
